decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Lotus Field and Scapeshift, while also utilizing land synergy and creature support to build up a strong board presence. By utilizing cards like The Wandering Minstrel and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods, the player can control the battlefield and set up for powerful combo plays. With cards like Terror of the Peaks and Kenrith, the Returned King as finishers, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with high-powered creatures and devastating combos.

This UW Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage and controlling the board. The deck utilizes counterspells like Dovin's Veto and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, along with removal spells like Supreme Verdict and Erode. Narset, Parter of Veils helps with card advantage, while cards like Consult the Star Charts and March of Otherworldly Light help with filtering and card draw. The deck also includes lands that can provide additional utility, such as Field of Ruin for disrupting the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with spells and efficiently bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard to the battlefield. This strategy involves a combination of cheap spells like Fiery Impulse and Opt to trigger Arclight Phoenix's ability, along with cards like Consider and Treasure Cruise to fuel the graveyard. The deck also includes disruption spells like Spell Pierce and Brazen Borrower to control the opponent's plays, while utilizing cards like Prismari Charm and Temporal Trespass for additional value and card advantage.

This Aggro Arclight Phoenix deck focuses on quickly casting spells and filling up the graveyard to bring back Arclight Phoenix. Cards like Consider, Opt, Lightning Axe, and Treasure Cruise help with card selection, drawing, and discarding to efficiently bring back Arclight Phoenix multiple times in a game. Additional burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Torch the Tower provide removal and direct damage to quickly close out the game. The deck also includes cards like Picklock Prankster and Ledger Shredder to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain hand advantage.

Utilizing a combo strategy involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, this deck focuses on generating value through sacrificing creatures and triggering powerful effects. With cards like Cache Grab and Formidable Speaker to help set up the combo, and disruptive spells like Thoughtseize to control the game, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its synergistic interactions. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk provide additional threats and versatility to the deck. Overall, this deck seeks to create a powerful engine that can quickly take over the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's strategy and ensure the combo can be assembled successfully. The deck also includes defensive creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia to stall the game until the combo can be executed. Monument to Endurance and Lively Dirge provide additional support for the combo, while removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse help control the board. Overall, the deck aims to win through a powerful combo while controlling the game state.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, hand disruption with Thoughtseize and Duress, and creatures like Tasigur and Sentinel of the Nameless City. It also has a strong graveyard presence with cards like Sheoldred and Scavenging Ooze. The mana base is centered around green and black sources, with utility lands like Castle Locthwain and Mutavault. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing its own threats on the board.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain board control by utilizing a combination of cheap spells and efficient creature removal. The deck focuses on exploiting tempo advantages by deploying threats quickly and disrupting opponents' strategies with countermagic and bounce spells. By leveraging powerful creatures like Gran-Gran and Vivi Ornitier alongside versatile spells like Consider and Divide by Zero, the deck aims to apply consistent pressure while maintaining a strong defensive game plan. Ultimately, the goal is to overwhelm opponents with a mix of aggression and disruption to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on aggressively playing Arclight Phoenix as early as possible, while using cheap spells like Opt, Fiery Impulse, and Academic Dispute to trigger its ability. Thing in the Ice provides another threat while also bouncing your opponent's creatures. The deck also includes card draw spells like Consider and Treasure Cruise to help maintain card advantage. Otawara, Soaring City and Picklock Prankster offer additional support, while Traumatic Critique disrupts the opponent's game plan. The land base includes the necessary dual lands to ensure smooth mana production.

This deck focuses on using the combination of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful win condition. By using cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Formidable Speaker to gather the necessary pieces, players can quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm their opponent. The deck also includes various support cards like Collective Brutality and Witherbloom Command to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, this deck aims to efficiently set up and execute its game-winning combo while maintaining flexibility and adaptability in different matchups.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and generate immense value through various synergies. The deck includes ways to protect its key combo pieces, disrupt the opponent's game plan with Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, and maintain card advantage with Fleeting Spirit and Geier Reach Sanitarium. The deck also includes various lands to support its strategy and ensure smooth mana base, making it a formidable force in the Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by disrupting opponents' strategies through cards like Thoughtseize, Duress, and Fatal Push, while also maintaining a strong board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Emeritus of Abundance. The inclusion of powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Boseiju, Who Endures ensures that the deck can also close out games quickly when needed. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's control elements and provide enough flexibility to handle a variety of situations.

The deck focuses on assembling the combination of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy. This involves protecting and supporting the combo pieces with cards like Fleeting Spirit, Iron-Shield Elf, and Monument to Endurance. Thoughtseize and Vanishing Verse are included to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while Fatal Push provides removal. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo pieces and ensure a smooth curve. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with its synergistic interactions.

This deck focuses on sacrificing creatures to trigger powerful abilities, dealing direct damage to the opponent while generating value through cards like Mayhem Devil and Cauldron Familiar. Its goal is to overwhelm the opponent quickly by utilizing efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Scavenger's Talent, while also maintaining a strong board presence with resilient creatures like Bloodtithe Harvester. The inclusion of versatile cards such as Deadly Dispute and Claim the Firstborn further enhances the deck's ability to disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory through aggressive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize spells that can trigger the Arclight Phoenix's ability to return from the graveyard to the battlefield and quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The deck focuses on card draw and efficient spell casting to set up powerful turns that can quickly close out the game. It is designed to be proactive and put pressure on the opponent while maintaining card advantage and tempo to stay ahead in the match.

The deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells and hand disruption to control the game while slowly grinding out value with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also utilizes lands that provide additional benefits such as Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures. By efficiently removing threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan, The Rock deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a battle of attrition.
